basics.name: J. Miguel Sánchez Almagro
basics.label: Software Engineer · Edge AI / ML
basics.summary: Software engineer with ~3 years across embedded automotive software (ex-BMW), backend development, and safety-critical software testing, plus a bilingual MSc in Cybersecurity. I build reliable systems close to the hardware and bring AI/ML into them — from on-device (edge) inference to LLM-powered backend services. Strong in C/C++ and Python; focused on embedded systems and backend (not front-end). In Australia on a Working Holiday visa — full work rights, available immediately.

work.name: Bertrandt (client: BMW)
work.summary: C++ embedded software for BMW's intelligent vehicle locking/unlocking feature (body / vehicle-access domain).
highlights: Developed and maintained C++ embedded software for BMW's intelligent locking/unlocking feature, from requirements through implementation and verification on the embedded target.
Worked in a Scrum team with daily feedback from the client (BMW); used Git and code review.

work.name: Bertrandt (client: Airbus)
work.summary: High-level testing of the cargo-section software of the Airbus A400M military transport aircraft.
highlights: Designed and executed test cases for the A400M cargo-section software and produced end-of-cycle certification documentation.
Worked to the DO-178 airborne-software standard with daily team meetings.

work.name: Capgemini
work.summary: Web application with a Java / Spring Boot backend and Angular front-end.
highlights: Built a web application with a Java / Spring Boot backend (Oracle SQL) and an Angular / TypeScript front-end, in a Full-Stack role focused on the backend.
Supervised code and coordinated the team for several weeks, in a Scrum environment.
Used Git / Bitbucket for version control and took part in reviews and the delivery cycle.

courses: Master's thesis: digital forensic traces left on a computer when a storage device is connected
Specialization in Artificial Intelligence, computing and video games
Strong base across programming, AI and operating systems (incl. embedded software)

projects.name: CAN-Bus Intrusion Detection (Edge ML)
projects.description: ML pipeline that detects automotive CAN-bus attacks (DoS / fuzzing / spoofing) with a C/C++ edge-inference component runnable in simulation — the trained model exported to run on microcontroller-class hardware. ~0.99 accuracy on the test set.

projects.name: AI Alert-Triage Backend
projects.description: FastAPI microservice that ingests security/telemetry events and uses an LLM (Claude) to triage them — severity, explanation, and recommended action — with a stub provider so it runs without an API key. Tested, containerized, CI.

meta.workRights: Working Holiday Visa (subclass 462) — full Australian work rights, available immediately, open to sponsored/permanent pathways.
